models.notes: A note is a markdown document created directly inside VaultAI by the user. It lives in a folder, can have tags, and accumulates AI-generated metadata over time.
models.flashcards: Flashcards are AI-generated question/answer pairs derived from a note's content to help the user retain key information.
models.insights: Key insights are AI-extracted bullet points summarizing the most important takeaways from a note.
models.relatedDocuments: Related documents are vault items (PDFs or other notes) that the AI identifies as conceptually linked to the current note.

relationships: Each Note belongs to one Folder
Each Note has many Flashcards generated from its content
Each Note has many Insights extracted by AI
Each Note has many Related Documents suggested by the AI

notes.title: Transformer Architecture — My Notes
notes.content:
# Transformer Architecture

The Transformer model was introduced in the paper *Attention Is All You Need* (Vaswani et al., 2017).

## Core Idea

Instead of processing sequences step-by-step like RNNs, Transformers process the entire sequence at once using **self-attention**.

## Self-Attention

For each token, self-attention computes a weighted sum of all other tokens:

```
Attention(Q, K, V) = softmax(QK^T / sqrt(d_k)) * V
```

## Why It Works

- Captures long-range dependencies efficiently
- Parallelizable — no sequential bottleneck
- Scales well with data and compute

## Key Components

1. Multi-head attention
2. Positional encoding
3. Feed-forward layers
4. Layer normalization

aiGenerated.summary: Personal study notes on the Transformer architecture, covering self-attention mechanics, positional encoding, and why the architecture outperforms RNNs for sequence tasks.
insights: Self-attention allows every token to attend to every other token in O(1) steps, unlike RNNs which require O(n)
The scaling factor sqrt(d_k) prevents vanishing gradients in the dot-product attention
Multi-head attention learns different relationship types in parallel — syntax, semantics, coreference
Positional encoding is necessary because attention is permutation-invariant by default

flashcards.question: What problem does self-attention solve that RNNs struggle with?
flashcards.answer: Long-range dependencies. RNNs degrade over long sequences due to vanishing gradients. Self-attention connects any two positions in O(1) operations.

flashcards.question: Why do we divide by sqrt(d_k) in the attention formula?
flashcards.answer: To prevent the dot products from growing too large in high dimensions, which would push softmax into regions with very small gradients.

flashcards.question: What is the purpose of positional encoding?
flashcards.answer: To inject sequence order information, since self-attention is permutation-invariant and cannot distinguish token positions on its own.

notes.title: VaultAI — Weekly Dev Log
notes.content:
# VaultAI Dev Log

## Week of Dec 7, 2025

Made good progress on the Rust embedding pipeline today. The `candle-rs` integration is working for BERT-style models.

### What worked
- Token chunking with overlap
- Batch embedding for multiple documents
- usearch index persistence

### Blockers
- PDF text extraction losing formatting on some docs
- Need to handle embedded images in PDFs separately

## Next Steps
- Wire up the Tauri IPC bridge for search
- Build the React search results component
- Test with larger document sets

notes.title: Ideas — Knowledge Graph UI
notes.content:
# Knowledge Graph UI Ideas

Thinking about how to visualize connections between documents...

- Force-directed graph using D3.js
- Nodes = documents, edges = semantic similarity score
- Color nodes by file type
- Click node to open document preview
- Zoom + pan navigation

notes.title: Reading Notes — Rust Ownership
notes.content:
# Rust Ownership Notes

Key rules:
1. Each value has one owner
2. When owner goes out of scope, value is dropped
3. You can have many immutable refs OR one mutable ref

## Lifetimes

Lifetimes ensure references don't outlive the data they point to...
